summary: Bake a Ken-Burns move onto a still (photo/logo) into a 9:16 cutaway clip: cover-crop or blurfill, one eased zoom/pan per preset.

parity.note: The b-roll TASK does not move: sourcing, the network fetch and the LLM tile-judge that PICK the still and its preset all stay on the brain (they need keys + net + the model). Only the ENCODE crosses, which is why op granularity beats task granularity here. The move's z/x/y expressions are a SINGLE SSOT shared by the CPU zoompan and the GPU libplacebo sampler, so the handler carries a byte-for-byte copy of the engine's Ken-Burns builders (its filtergraph module plus the shared GPU translator, which live in the engine tree, not this public repo), pinned BYTE-EQUAL in the engine's parity suite — the CPU filtergraph is reproduced bit-for-bit (today's b-roll forces the CPU path, so the migrated op is byte-identical to what shipped). It is nonetheless `perceptual`, not `bit_exact`, because the pod now renders the same move on GPU libplacebo (spline36 resample) where the box ran CPU zoompan (bilinear): identical geometry, different rasteriser. Intended GPU-vs-CPU bar: PSNR >= 38 dB / SSIM >= 0.98. The GPU render is UNPROVEN in a CPU-only environment with no Vulkan device — only the strings are pinned; a Vulkan-less box falls back to the pinned CPU path.

preset.description: WHICH move — the judge/router's decision. Named, not an expression: the z/x/y filtergraph is the algorithm and stays in the handler.

scale.description: How non-vertical footage fills 9:16. `cover` crops to fill (a photo that fills the frame); `blurfill` fits the WHOLE image on a blurred bg (a logo / small / square mark that must never be cropped or blown up).

amount.description: Peak zoom delta for in/out. A gentle 0.06 on a logo, 0.12-0.24 on a photo — the router's call, clamped to the frame.

pan_zoom.description: Constant zoom kept for pure pans so there is room to move.

cycles.description: breathe/zoom_pulse oscillation count across the clip.

dur.description: Clip seconds. Omit for a still to use the 3.5s default cutaway length.

seek.description: Seek into a video source (ignored for a looped still).

encode_profile.description: Named quality tier, NOT encoder flags — same SSOT pattern as scripts/render_profile.py. The intermediate cq/preset ladder is tuning and stays in the handler.
